title: Native Triangle vs. NES-Style Triangle
description: An A/B comparison between the browser's native triangle oscillator and the NES-style PeriodicWave used in Dead Beat and Falling Hall.
blurb: an A/B comparison between the browser's native triangle oscillator and the NES-style PeriodicWave used in Dead Beat and Falling Hall.
one_sentence_claim: A 32-step NES-style source table reconstructed as a 24-harmonic PeriodicWave is a different audio artifact from the browser's native triangle oscillator, and the contrast is audible when both pass through identical bell synthesis.
why_this_matters: The chewgum-dsp library exists because the NES-style PeriodicWave has a different audible character from the browser's native triangle oscillator. This page is the proof: same envelope, same filter, same scale, only the wave differs.
principle: two distinct triangle wave sources, isolated by holding everything else constant
what_changes_on_screen: Two buttons trigger a bell tone at a random MIDI note from the same scale; the only thing that changes between the two paths is the triangle wave source passed to strikeBell.
user_interaction: Click Ring (Native) or Ring (NES-Style); audio is dormant until first click, then a bell tone plays per click.

demo_parameters.label: Source library
demo_parameters.value: chewgum-dsp v0.1.0 (vendored inline on this page)

demo_parameters.label: NES-style wave
demo_parameters.value: 32-step source table -> 24-harmonic Web Audio PeriodicWave with DC offset removed

demo_parameters.label: Bell synthesis (shared)
demo_parameters.value: 3-voice (triangle fundamental + sine harmonics at x2.01 and x3.17), bandpass filter (Q 2.4, capped at 3200 Hz), 1.45 s primary decay

demo_parameters.label: Scale
demo_parameters.value: MIDI [69, 72, 76, 79, 81, 84] -- one note picked per click

validation_idea: Listen on both buttons with the same fixed master gain; the contrast must be audible to a human reviewer.

source_trail.text: Library: chewgum-dsp v0.1.0 on GitHub. Source for createNesTriangleWave, midiToFrequency, and strikeBell.
NES-style PeriodicWave deployed in Dead Beat (bell tones).
NES-style PeriodicWave deployed in Falling Hall (wall-strike chimes).
Native triangle oscillator deployed in Phosphor (contrast point, not a deployment of this library).

preferred_citation: Shane Curry, "Native Triangle vs. NES-Style Triangle," https://shanecurry.com/lab/toys/triangle-engines/, published 2026-04-29.
